About Insights Free Tool The Audit
Private brief · Prepared for Sean Levy · May 2026

You rank better than every competitor in your category. Your traffic is zero. Here is the honest read on why, and what Wincher cannot show you about it.

00 Why this exists

Sean. Lance. Good to be back in touch. Macy's Orlando feels like a long time ago and somehow not.

You saw the Never Ranked post and asked me to take a look at twspaperie.com, and you shared the Wincher report you have running on the site (April 6 to May 5, 26 keywords). I went through it the way I look at every site we audit, and the numbers tell a specific story I want to walk you through. The comparison to Wincher writes itself from the data.

This brief is long because the category is new and I would rather give you the whole picture once than nibble at it over text. What your own Wincher data is telling you. What Wincher tracks vs what is actually deciding TWS Paperie's revenue. An honest side-by-side. When Wincher is the right tool. When Never Ranked is. And what to do next.

01 Both tools, in one paragraph each

Wincher is a Google rank tracker. It watches where your pages sit in Google's blue-link results for the keywords you choose, charts how those positions move over time, and flags wins and losses against your competitors. It is genuinely good at that job. The PDF you sent me is clean, the data is honest, and the keyword grouping by search intent is well done. As a Google-rankings dashboard, Wincher does what it says.

Never Ranked is a different product for a different problem. We do not track Google blue-link rankings. We track whether your business gets cited when someone asks ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Google AI Overviews, Claude, or Microsoft Copilot a question your buyer is asking. We deploy the schema markup that makes a small business citable in those answers (Restaurant, LocalBusiness, Service, Product, Offer, OpeningHours, Review) directly to your site through a one-line snippet, refresh dynamic content daily, and prove the citation lift with statistical attribution. Pricing: $750 one-time audit (credited toward the first month if you continue), then Pulse at $497 per month, Signal at $2,000 per month, or Amplify at $4,500 per month. Both tools are real. They solve different problems. Wincher tells you where you stand on Google. Never Ranked changes whether you appear in the AI answer that is increasingly replacing the Google click.

02 Side by side

Quick reference. The differences that matter most for a small-business owner deciding which problem to spend on.

Capability Wincher Never Ranked
Tracks Google blue-link rankingsYes (the core product)No (we leave that to a tool like Wincher)
Tracks AI engine citationsNoYes, six engines
Engines trackedGoogle onlyChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Google AI Overviews, Claude, Microsoft Copilot
Explains a "ranking #1, traffic 0" paradoxShows position only. Cannot tell you whether an AI Overview is intercepting the click.Shows which engines answered the query, who got cited, and where you sit relative to the cited domains.
Schema markup deploymentNoYes. One-line snippet, works on Squarespace, Shopify, WooCommerce, WordPress, custom CMS, anything.
Reddit citation trackingNoYes (most "best of" answers from AI engines pull from Reddit)
Generates content drafts from your siteNoFAQ, BreadcrumbList, and Article schemas auto-generated from your actual page content. You review every draft before it ships.
Daily refresh of dynamic contentNoYes. Products, events, seasonal categories stay synced.
Sentiment within citationsNoEvery cited response auto-classified. Negative mentions flagged the moment they appear.
Real-time alertsPosition-change email summariesCitation gain or loss alerts emailed within hours, color-coded
Variant impact attributionNoYes. Citation rate measured 4 weeks before deploy vs 4 weeks after, two-proportion z-test, statistical confidence at p < 0.05.
Free public scorerLimited free tiercheck.neverranked.com
Codebase publicly inspectableNogithub.com/LanceRoylo/neverranked
Entry priceFrom around $20 per month for keyword rank tracking$750 one-time audit, then $497 per month (Pulse) for ongoing
03 When Wincher is the right call

Honestly. Keep Wincher when:

  • The keyword set you care about still drives clicks from Google's blue-link results, and you have the budget for paid Google Ads on those same terms (rankings still matter to you, even if AI is eating part of the share).
  • You want a low-cost dashboard to monitor positions month over month and you have the time to act on what you see (the Wincher tier you are on is hard to beat for the price as a position tracker).
  • You are still in the phase of growing organic visibility for "luxury wrapping paper" type top-of-funnel queries where Google still drives most of the traffic. Wincher is fine for that.
  • You want a free tier to keep an eye on a small keyword set without committing to anything bigger.

Wincher is doing its job. The PDF is well organized. The keyword grouping is clean. As a Google rank tracker, it is worth what you are paying for it. The argument is not "drop Wincher." The argument is "Wincher is showing you a slice of the picture that is shrinking, and the slice that is growing is invisible to it."

04 When Never Ranked is the right call (the case for TWS Paperie)

Add Never Ranked when:

  • Your data already proves Google rank does not equal traffic. The Wincher report shows TWS Paperie at average position 15.1 (the best of any competitor in the report) and estimated traffic of zero. Casparionline ranks at 28.8 average and gets 72 estimated visits. Riflepaperco ranks at 26.2 and gets 44. Worse rankings, more traffic. The reason is what the buyer is actually doing on the AI side, and Wincher cannot show it to you.
  • Your buyer is asking AI before they search. A bride in Atlanta visiting her sister in Orlando asks Siri "where to get a wedding gift wrapped in Orlando." A mom planning a baby shower asks ChatGPT "best gift wrapping for a baby shower in Central Florida." If TWS Paperie is not in those answers, the customer never reaches your site, and your Google ranking does not save you.
  • Your inventory changes (categories, product lines, seasonal pages) and AI engines need to see it as structured data, not as a Squarespace block of text. The /brown-paper-packages/, /book-now/, /product-category/gift-wrap/, /product-category/gift-wrap/christmas/ pages all exist and rank, but none of them publish Restaurant, LocalBusiness, Service, OpeningHours, Offer, or AggregateRating schema in machine-readable form. AI engines cannot read them confidently enough to cite.
  • You want someone to ship the fixes, not describe them. You run a small business. Schema markup is not a thing you want to learn. We deploy it through a snippet, validate it before it goes live, and prove the citation lift four weeks later.
  • You want public proof, not a black box. The codebase is public. The schema scorer is public. The security model is public. You can hand any of it to a developer and get an honest second opinion before signing anything.

The Hawaii Theatre case study below is what an engagement looks like in practice. Different category, same playbook.

05 What this looks like in practice (Hawaii Theatre, 24 hours)

Different vertical from yours, same shape of problem. Hawaii Theatre Center signed with us April 24, 2026. Their AEO Readiness Score on the homepage was 45 of 100, Grade D. They had no schema markup of any kind on a site that does 80,000 to 100,000 patron visits a year. Five red flags. Zero structured data.

Yesterday, May 1, we deployed 36 schemas in one work session:

  • Foundation. PerformingArtsTheater, WebSite, AggregateRating wired to their actual 4.6 from 700 Google reviews.
  • Inventory. 31 Event schemas, one per active show, scoped to /upcoming-events. Pulled from their public events page automatically.
  • Navigation. 2 BreadcrumbList schemas, scoped per section.
  • Editorial. 1 FAQPage schema generated from their actual homepage content. 8 questions, all factual, all approved by their team before going live.

Score moved from D 45 to A 95 in less than 24 hours. Six of twenty-six roadmap items checked off. Daily Event refresh now runs automatically every morning at 6am UTC. When a show sells out and drops off the page, its schema deactivates the next morning. Their team does nothing.

That is the structural difference. A rank tracker would show "their visibility moved." We shipped the fixes that caused it. Public proof at github.com/LanceRoylo/neverranked. The actual schemas serving live at app.neverranked.com/inject/hawaii-theatre.json.

For TWS Paperie the playbook would be the same shape but tuned to your inventory. Restaurant or LocalBusiness schema for "gift wrapping orlando" type queries. Service schema for the wrapping appointments and event-gifting work. Product or Offer schema templated across the gift-wrap categories. OpeningHours so "is gift wrapping open right now in Orlando" gets a confident answer. AggregateRating so "is TWS Paperie worth it" pulls from a rating you stand behind. Plus the FAQPage schema generated from your own copy and reviewed before it ships.

06 Pricing, side by side
Tool Tier Price What's included
WincherFree$05 keywords, daily updates, basic position tracking
WincherStarter / ProFrom around $20 to $80 per month (varies by keyword volume)Up to 100 to 500 keywords, daily updates, competitor tracking, SERP features, search intent classification
WincherBusiness / AgencyHigher tiersMultiple sites, larger keyword volumes, white-label
Never RankedAudit$750 one-timeFull scan, 90-day roadmap, competitor citation matrix, schema fix code. Credited toward first month of any tier within 30 days.
Never RankedPulse$497 / moEntry tier: monthly Visibility Index PDF, 10 tracked prompts, 2 schemas deployed monthly, 6-engine tracking, multi-pass validation
Never RankedSignal$2,000 / moAudit + ongoing schema deploy + 6-engine citation tracking + real-time alerts + Reddit + authority + drift detection + variant impact attribution
Never RankedAmplify$4,500 / moSignal + content writing + auto-publish + Reddit reply briefs + brand voice fingerprint

Honest framing for a small business decision. Wincher costs less because it does less. It tracks Google rankings. The Pulse tier at $497 per month is the lowest meaningful tier from us because the work below that is just visibility data, and the visibility data is not the bottleneck for TWS Paperie. The bottleneck is the schema deployment and the AI engine citation work. Pulse gives you both, plus a monthly Visibility Index PDF that shows movement across all six engines. Signal at $2,000 and Amplify at $4,500 are the deeper engagements where we ship more schema and (Amplify) the content drafts. The way I think about it for a small business is: Wincher stays for the rank tracking it is good at, and a Pulse subscription buys you the answer to "why is the rank not turning into traffic anymore." If the answer pays back inside ninety days through one or two new wedding or event clients that found you through AI, the unit economics work.

07 Why NeverRanked, honestly

The fair pitch. As of May 2026, Never Ranked is the only AEO platform in market that ships all of the following together:

  • Six-engine citation tracking: Perplexity, ChatGPT, Gemini, Google AI Overviews (live via DataForSEO), Claude, and Microsoft Copilot via Bing (added this week). No other AEO platform tracks all six. Profound and Athena cover three to four. Ahrefs Brand Radar covers four. We are the only ones at six.
  • Done-for-you schema deployment via a one-line snippet. No CMS access, no dev cycle. Profound, Athena HQ, Ahrefs Brand Radar all stop at "here is what to deploy." We deploy it. Wincher is in this gap too. It shows you positions. It does not change them.
  • Multi-pass AI validation on every generated output. Factual grounding against the source, tone check, quality gate, three-attempt regeneration. Most "AI content" tools ship Claude's first draft. We refuse to.
  • Variant impact attribution: every schema we deploy is tracked as a variant. Citation rate measured 4 weeks before deploy vs 4 weeks after, two-proportion z-test, statistical confidence at p < 0.05. The monthly Visibility Index includes a "what we deployed and what it did" section. Nobody else does this.
  • Prominence and sentiment within citations, not just yes/no. Where in the AI's answer were you mentioned (rank 1 hero quote vs rank 8 footnote)? Was the tone positive or negative? That's the next layer of AEO that most platforms have not built yet.
  • Real-time citation alerts on Signal+: gained or lost citations between monthly reports get emailed within hours, color-coded by gain or loss. The buyer hears about movement before competitors do.

Honest framing. Never Ranked is bootstrapped, founder-led, and currently has a small customer count. That is the buying advantage at the Pulse tier. Pilot pricing, direct founder access, and the ability to shape the roadmap with an actual small-business voice in the room. As the platform grows, the tier you start at locks in.

08 What to do next

This brief was the look you asked for. Two ways forward if it is useful, both async, both at your pace.

Run a free scan on twspaperie.com →
Paste your domain. Takes ten seconds. You get the schema score, the missing pieces, and a per-engine grade. Same scorer that ran on Hawaii Theatre. No signup, no email gate.

Or let me run the full audit. On me, because you asked.
The version of this brief that lists at $750 is yours, no charge. Six-engine citation matrix on the Orlando gift-wrapping queries (custom gift wrapping central florida, gift wrapping near orlando, holiday gift wrapping orlando, luxury gift wrapping orlando, plus anything you tell me to add). Per-page schema completeness report on twspaperie.com. Competitor map, named domains, named threads. A 90-day implementation roadmap with the JSON-LD code your developer or your Squarespace can paste in. Delivered as a PDF in 48 hours, no meeting required. Reply with the queries you want included and a green light, I will start.

One thing to know going in. The audit is a snapshot. AEO is a moving target. AI engines retrain on a cycle, your competitors are deploying their own schema (the smart ones already are), and the answer to "best gift wrapping in Orlando" today is not the same answer six weeks from now. The audit shows you the state of play. Pulse, Signal, and Amplify (starting at $497 per month) are what actually hold the position once you have it. If the audit makes the case, the recurring work is where the value compounds. If it does not, you got an honest read out of an old friend at no cost. Both outcomes are fine.

Or just reply with thoughts. No agenda, no calendar invite, no screen share. If a question is bugging you after you read this, the email is below.

Good to be back in touch, Sean. Hope the wrapping season treats you well.
